parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: Your class has attended a seminar about where a government's sports budget would be best spent. You have made the notes below. Write an essay discussing two of the ideas in your notes. You should explain which idea would bring the greater benefit to the country, giving reasons to support your opinion.

stimulus.title: Where would a government's sports budget be best spent?
stimulus.body:
• funding elite athletes to win international medals
• building everyday facilities such as pools and pitches
• improving physical education in schools

Some opinions expressed in the seminar:
"One gold medal does more for national pride than a hundred new pitches."
"Medals inspire people for a week; a local pool changes their whole life."
"If children learn to hate sport at school, no stadium will win them back."

bullets: explain which would bring the greater benefit
give reasons to support your opinion

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: Your town council is deciding how to spend its sports budget and has asked residents' groups for reports on local facilities.

stimulus.title: Report: sports facilities in the town
stimulus.body: Write a report describing which sports facilities residents currently use and which they avoid, explaining the main reasons for their choices, and recommending where the council's money would make the biggest difference.

bullets: which facilities residents use and avoid
the main reasons for their choices
where the money would make the biggest difference

tasks.lead: An English-language sports website has invited reviews of sports documentaries.

stimulus.title: Sports documentary reviews
stimulus.body: Review a documentary about a sportsperson, team or sporting event. Describe the story it tells, assess whether it offers real insight or simply celebrates its subject, and say whether someone with no interest in sport would still enjoy it.

bullets: whether it offers real insight or just celebration
whether non-sports fans would enjoy it

tasks.lead: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend Leo.

stimulus.body:
From: Leo
Subject: Coming for the championships!

Guess what - I've got tickets for the international championships being held in your country next year! I'll be there for a week, but I only have tickets for two days of events.

What's the atmosphere likely to be like? How should I plan the days around the sport, and is there anything about how your country celebrates these occasions that I should know about before I arrive?

Leo

bullets: what the atmosphere will be like
how Leo should plan the non-sport days
what Leo should know about how your country celebrates
